author: giblicious
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. Rich text editing mode (WYSIWYG). Focus on writing, not syntax.

manifest.author: Spreadwell
manifest.description: WYSIWYG editing mode. Renders Markdown in place; files stay canonical.
manifest.name: Butter Editor

name: Butter Editor

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.10.1

- Fixed copy-paste losing wikilinks, tags, and embeds (pasted as standard links instead of Obsidian syntax).
- Fixed list buttons missing from the mobile toolbar.
- Fixed UI elements (drag handles, selection ring) painting over the toolbar on scroll.
- Fixed new-note creation showing a naming popup instead of focusing the inline title.
- Fixed toolbar customizer drag becoming unstable during fast moves.
- Fixed tag pills getting clipped at the bottom.
- Fixed divider block drag handle not centering on the line.
- Added list indentation guides toggle in settings.
- Added frontmatter visibility toggle command (command palette).
- Toolbar forced to top-attached position when the license banner is showing.
- Cleaner language across all settings, modals, and web pages.
- Licensing hardening: local trial expiry check, forced revalidation on wake, upgrade polling survives closing settings.
- Relaxed rate limits for smoother checkout flow.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Butter Editor 0.10.0

- Licenses now activate automatically after purchase. No key to copy or paste.
- New frontmatter visibility setting: always show, always hide, or match Obsidian.
- Redesigned license indicator for read-only mode.
- Reworked block drag engine.
- Improved trial and purchase flow.
- Misc bug fixes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Butter Editor 0.9.9

- Strike-through on checked task-list items now uses the standard `text-decoration: line-through` shorthand without a separate decoration color. Closes a directory-review CSS-lint warning. Visually, the strike-through now matches the muted text color of the rest of the checked-item line instead of being a touch lighter.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Butter Editor 0.9.8

- `authorUrl` in the manifest now points at the Spreadwell GitHub profile instead of the plugin's own repo. Addresses an Obsidian directory review warning. No user-visible change.
- Release artifacts (`main.js`, `styles.css`) now ship with GitHub build-provenance attestations. Anyone downloading from BRAT or the directory can cryptographically verify the files came from this repo at this commit.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Butter Editor 0.9.7

- Plugin author renamed to "Spreadwell" so the plugin browser shows "Butter Editor by Spreadwell" instead of "Butter Editor by Butter Editor". No behavior change.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Butter Editor 0.9.6

- Save failures now show a notice when something blocks the write (disk full, sync lock, dropped
  network drive). Previously they failed silently.
- Third-party plugins that throw while rendering inside Butter no longer take the view down with them. Their errors land in Butter's error log.
- Two commands renamed: `Toggle Butter Editor mode` is now `Toggle WYSIWYG mode`. `Open current note
  in Butter Editor` is now `Open current note in WYSIWYG view`. Re-bind shortcuts if you had them on either.
- Minimum Obsidian version is now 1.7.2.
- Disable / uninstall now leaves no trace behind.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Butter Editor 0.9.5

- **Source is now public.** Butter has moved from closed-source to source-available proprietary. The full plugin source lives in this repo so you can read it, audit it, and learn from it. The new [LICENSE](../LICENSE) restricts redistribution as a competing general-purpose Obsidian plugin, but doesn't restrict private use, reading, or learning from the code.
- **Why this matters for you.** Source-available is the path into the official Obsidian community plugin directory, so Butter can stop being BRAT-only and reach users who don't install third-party plugins. It also means anyone can verify what Butter does with your notes (the answer is still: edits them in place, never transmits content anywhere).
- **No behavior changes in this release.** 0.9.5 is the licensing/distribution switchover. Editor functionality is identical to 0.9.4.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Butter Editor 0.9.4

- **Text color and highlight color.** New toolbar buttons let you color text and apply colored highlights. The highlight tints stay translucent so colored text underneath stays visible. Don't want HTML in your files? Turn off Settings → Behavior → HTML formatting and the buttons hide.
- **Clear formatting button.** Strips bold, italic, color, highlight, and other styling from your selection in one click.
- **Welcome walkthrough refresh.** Friendlier first-time setup, with a built-in trial offer.
- **License banner reskin.** The "license required" notice now sits alongside the toolbar instead of floating as a card above your note.
- **Drag handles return after Obsidian reload.** Fixes a bug where block drag handles vanished until you closed and reopened the tab.
- **Better keyboard and screen-reader support.** Arrow keys walk the toolbar, slash menu, and table-size picker. License and save-status changes are announced.
- **Security hardening.** Wikilinks and links can no longer carry `javascript:` schemes; embed source is sanitised before render.
